Baby Name Browser

Browse 100,000+ real recorded baby names alphabetically. Filter by letter and gender, then tap any name for full popularity statistics, meaning and decade trends.

Looking for rare names only? Unique Name Generator. Want random suggestions? Random Name Generator.

AaronAbigailAdamAdrianAidenAlanAlbertAlexAlexanderAlexandraAliceAliciaAllenAllisonAmandaAmberAmyAndreaAndrewAngelaAnnAnnaAnneAnthonyAntonioAprilAriaArianaArthurAshleyAudreyAuroraAustinAveryAva

What is the Baby Name Browser?

The Baby Name Browser is a free A–Z directory of 100,000+ real recorded baby names. Click any letter to see all names beginning with it, filter by gender, then tap any name for its full popularity statistics, decade trend, meaning and origin. Every name is sourced from official birth registries from the US Social Security Administration and UK ONS — no AI-invented strings. Use this tool if you're exploring options, or head home to find out how many people have your name.

Key Features and Attributes

A–Z letter navigation

26 one-click letter buttons to jump anywhere in the alphabet.

Gender filter

Boy, Girl, or Any (includes unisex).

100k+ name database

Real birth-registry names from US, UK, Canada, Australia and 78 other countries.

Full stats per name

Click any name to see rank, bearer count, decade chart, meaning and origin.

Mobile-friendly grid

Responsive layout adapts from 2 columns on mobile to 3 on desktop.

Zero signup

Browse unlimited names without account or email.

Who Uses This Tool?

Parents building a shortlist

Scenario: Want to scan all 'M' names systematically.

Result: Click M, scroll through every recorded M name, drill into the ones that catch your eye.

Genealogists verifying spellings

Scenario: Researching whether 'Mildred' or 'Millicent' was more common in the 1920s.

Result: Browse the M list, tap each, compare decade charts side by side.

Authors picking period names

Scenario: Need a list of all 'E' girl names that existed in 1880.

Result: Filter to girl names, click E, cross-reference with the decade trend on each.

Curious browsers

Scenario: Want to discover names they've never heard of.

Result: Pick a less-common letter (Q, X, Y) and find unusual options instantly.

Worked Examples

Input: "A + Girl"

Finding: Returned 30+ names: Aaliyah, Abigail, Adelaide, Aria, Aurora…

Insight: A is the largest girl-name letter — many top-100 names start with A.

Input: "X + Any"

Finding: Returned: Xavier, Ximena, Xander

Insight: X is one of the rarest starting letters — only 3 names cracking common usage.

Input: "M + Boy"

Finding: Returned: Marcus, Mason, Matthew, Maxwell, Miles, Muhammad…

Insight: M dominates classic and modern boy naming traditions across cultures.

✓ Pros

  • ✓ Complete A–Z directory in one place
  • ✓ Real-name database, no hallucinations
  • ✓ Free + unlimited
  • ✓ Mobile-responsive grid
  • ✓ One click to full statistics page

✗ Cons

  • ✗ No popularity-based sorting yet (alphabetical only)
  • ✗ Cannot bulk-favorite names (shortlist coming)
  • ✗ Less coverage for non-Western naming traditions

Frequently Asked Questions

How do I browse baby names alphabetically?▾

Click any letter A–Z above to view all names beginning with that letter. Use the gender dropdown to narrow further.

Are these all the baby names that exist?▾

We cover 100,000+ recorded names from US SSA, UK ONS, and 78 other registries. New names are added each year as new birth records are released.

How are baby names sorted?▾

Names within each letter are sorted alphabetically by default. Click any name to drill into its full statistics including popularity and meaning.

Can I filter by gender?▾

Yes — choose Boy, Girl, or Any. The Any filter includes unisex names like Quinn, River and Sage.

Do you cover names from non-English cultures?▾

Yes. The database includes Spanish, Indian, Arabic, Chinese, African and European names. English names are best-covered today; others expand each year.

Why are some letters smaller than others?▾

X, Q, Y, U and Z naturally have fewer baby names. A, J, M, S and L have the largest catalogues, reflecting historical naming patterns.

Can I see meanings on the browse page?▾

Tap any name to open its dedicated page with meaning, origin, popularity rank and decade trend.

Is the Baby Name Browser free?▾

Yes — unlimited browsing, no signup, no email, no paywall.

Are baby name lists updated?▾

Yes, the dataset refreshes annually with the previous year's official birth registry release.

Can I save my favourite names?▾

A 'favourites' shortlist is on the roadmap. For now, bookmark individual name pages or copy the URL.

Baby Name Browser data sourced from U.S. SSA, UK ONS, and 78 additional countries. Last updated: Q1 2025.
